body:
]> 16/07/2026 7:35 Mt Perisher 6 is Open, plus lifts in all four resort areas spinning!.

Good morning and happy Thursday from Perisher. We are in for another stellar day on the mountain with clear skies and light winds. The forecast is calling for a top of 8 degrees, with winds southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h tending south to southeasterly 15 to 25 km/h in the morning.

With temperatures dropping to below zero and low humidity, the snowguns fired up at around 5pm and are still running this morning.

With MtP6 opening yesterday afternoon, alongside Ridge Chair in Blue Cow and the Blue Calf T-Bar in Guthega, we are expecting 26 lifts to spin today. Conditions will be firm and fast again today, with man-made snow topping up the base. We have 30 groomed runs across the resort. We'd recommend waiting for sunny aspects off-piste to soften before getting too adventurous.

The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. Keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates, and make sure you've got notifications turned on in the Perisher App, we'll ping you the moment major lifts come online.

A few good things to know: when a lift is opening for the first time this season, our lift crews and ski patrol need time to prep the lift and terrain, and run full safety checks before anyone can ride. It's all in the name of keeping the mountain safe for everyone, so please respect run closed signs and give the team the space to get it done properly.

Congratulations to everyone who has competed in Sydney Interschools over the past couple of days, we're into the third day with racing on Front Valley and Leichhardt again. For entertainment, we've got live music at Jax Bar from midday today and from 1-3pm at Blue Cow Bistro.

]]> weather-blank Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

]]> Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

]]> Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> 72 0 33 26 202 30 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
